channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Balancing Local Aviation Goals with Global Financing in Nigeria
item.description: Nigeria's aviation sector faces a tough balancing act between boosting local participation and maintaining access to global financing. The Fly Nigeria Bill aims to redirect government travel spending ...

item.title: Shiv Sena Corporator Ramesh Mhatre Denies Assault on Female Doctors at Hospital
item.description: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre has denied allegations of assaulting female doctors at Shashtri Nagar Hospital, stating he did not raise his hand against them. The incident, captured in a viral vid...

item.title: Female Students Confront Man Found in Ladies' Hostel at Kenyan University
item.description: Female students at a Kenyan university confronted and attacked a man they caught inside their hostel after he allegedly tried to lock their roommate in a room. The incident, captured on video, shows t...

item.title: Ventura Teen Charged with Felonies After Fleeing Police on Freeway
item.description: A 17-year-old resident of Ventura, California, has been charged with two felony counts after allegedly fleeing police officers by riding an electric motorcycle the wrong way on a highway. According to...

item.title: Cargo Plane Lost Over Arabian Sea; Search and Rescue Operations Intensified
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has directed the Civil Aviation Authority (CAA), Pakistan Navy, and Pakistan Air Force (PAF) to intensify search and rescue operations in the Arabian Sea following the di...

item.title: Iran launches retaliatory attacks on US bases in Bahrain and Kuwait after renewed American strikes near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran came under renewed strain after Iran launched retaliatory attacks against American military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait following fresh US militar...

item.title: Sudan's Poverty Crisis Deepens as Conflict Escalates and Humanitarian Needs Surge
item.description: The UN warns that Sudan's poverty rate has jumped from 45% to 73% since the war began in April 2023, leaving millions struggling to meet basic needs. Rising inflation, a weak currency, and soaring liv...

item.title: Seasonal road trips gaining popularity among Australians
item.description: More Australians are embracing domestic road trips over overseas holidays due to cost-effectiveness, flexibility, and the unique seasonal experiences Australia offers. Seasonal travel allows families ...

item.title: Avoiding AI Scams: The Importance of Not Answering Phones with 'Hello' First
item.description: The article discusses the growing threat of artificial intelligence-driven scams targeting individuals through phone calls. Author Rachel Rickard Strauss highlights how scammers are using advanced tec...

item.title: Financial Trust and Partnership in Times of Crisis: Balancing Support and Independence
item.description: The article explores a survey on whether individuals should use their emergency funds to support a financially struggling partner. It highlights a split in opinions: 62% believe transparency and suppo...

item.title: Tanzania to Establish Real Estate Regulatory Authority to Improve Sector Standards
item.description: The Tanzanian government is set to establish a Real Estate Regulatory Authority to professionalize the property market and curb fraudulent practices. The planned authority will regulate brokers and ag...

item.title: Fact-Check: Videos Misrepresent Nepal Protests as Philippine Rallies
item.description: The article fact-checks videos that falsely claimed large crowds in the Philippines protested corruption in early July 2026. These videos, posted on July 1, 2026, were part of the Iglesia ni Cristo (I...

item.title: Father of Chakwal CCD Victim Claims Police Forged Paper and Misrepresented Incident
item.description: The article details the tragic incident in Chakwal where nine-year-old Hania Ahmed, an Australian citizen, was killed during a robbery. Her father, Adeel Ahmed, alleges that the police misrepresented ...

item.title: Halifax Costco Hate Crime: Man Assaults Family, Yells Racial Slurs
item.description: A man allegedly yelled racial slurs and made threatening remarks toward a family shopping at a Halifax Costco before assaulting another customer, according to police. The incident occurred on July 4 a...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Exchange Military Strikes After Attacks on Ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iran have escalated tensions by engaging in a series of military strikes following attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The conflict, which marks a significant ...

item.title: Jackson Connor Reflects on Role in Groundbreaking LGBTQ+ Drama 'Tip Toe'
item.description: Jackson Connor, 23, discusses his role as George Goss in the critically acclaimed LGBTQ+ drama 'Tip Toe', created by Russell T Davies. The series, streaming on Channel 4, explores themes of queer iden...

item.title: Marie Spartali Stillman: Pioneering Female Artist of the Pre-Raphaelite Movement
item.description: Marie Spartali Stillman (1844–1927) was a British painter of Greek descent and one of the most accomplished female artists associated with the second generation of the Pre-Raphaelite movement. Born in...

item.title: Israel's Electric Vehicle Market Slows Below Global Average
item.description: Israel's electric vehicle market has declined below the global average of 14%, with EVs accounting for 11% of new car sales in 2026. This marks a sharp reversal from previous years of rapid growth, wh...

item.title: Borno Gov approves N1.65bn for 300 CJTF orphans' education
item.description: Borno State Governor, Prof. Babagana Umara Zulum, has approved releasing N1.649 billion to settle outstanding scholarship obligations for 300 orphans of Counter-Insurgency Volunteers and other student...

item.title: Niagara Falls Mayor Cites Public Opposition and Private Sector Offer as Observation Wheel Proposal Faces Cancellation
item.description: The controversial plan by the Ford government to build a second giant observation wheel in Queen Victoria Park, Niagara Falls, has faced significant opposition. Mayor Jim Diodati of Niagara Parks Comm...
